'''Marc Sneyd''' (born 9 February 1991) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/player/10625 |title=Super League |access-date=6 May 2013 |archive-date=7 August 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130807035157/http://superleague.co.uk/player/10625 |url-status=live }}</ref>

He made over 160 Super League appearances for [[Hull F.C.]], where he won two [[Challenge Cup]] finals, and also previously spent a season on loan from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He played as a {{rlp|so}} and {{rlp|fb}} earlier in his career.

==Background==
Sneyd was born in [[Oldham]], [[Greater Manchester]], England, and grew up in Shaw.<ref name="YP16Apr19" /> He played junior rugby league for Waterhead Warriors.<ref>{{cite web |title=Home-grown talents reach for the stars |url=https://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/9/sport-news/36185/homegrown-talents-reach-for-the-stars |website=Oldham Evening Chronicle |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=3 February 2010}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Salford===
He came through the junior ranks at [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref name="BBC7May13">{{cite web |title=Salford: Darrell Griffin completes move as Marc Sneyd stays |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/22440849 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=7 May 2013 |quote=Scrum-half Sneyd, 22, has progressed through the academy... |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707141025/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/22440849 |url-status=live }}</ref> He made his début for Salford in 2010 but his game time was limited.<ref name="BBC1Jul14" /> In May 2013, he signed a new two-year contract with Salford.<ref name="SS7May13">{{cite web |title=Utility back Marc Sneyd signs new two-year deal at Salford City Reds |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/8697457/Utility-back-Marc-Sneyd-signs-new-two-year-deal-at-Salford-City-Reds |website=Sky Sports |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=7 May 2013 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707224218/https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/8697457/Utility-back-Marc-Sneyd-signs-new-two-year-deal-at-Salford-City-Reds |url-status=live }}</ref>

===Castleford===
Sneyd joined [[Castleford Tigers]] on loan for the 2014 season.<ref name="SS13Sep13">{{cite web |title=Super League: Marc Sneyd joins Castleford on season-long loan |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/8918551 |website=Sky Sports |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=13 September 2013 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707224219/https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/8918551 |url-status=live }}</ref> Castleford coach [[Daryl Powell]] described him as "...a quality player with the added benefit of an outstanding left-foot kicking game.”<ref name="SS13Sep13"/> This was Sneyd's breakthrough season in which he scored 7 tries and kicked 117 goals in just 31 appearances, and appeared in the [[2014 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall’s double stuns Castleford">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Leeds lift Challenge Cup after Ryan Hall's double stuns Castleford|publisher=Guardian|date=23 August 2014|access-date=13 November 2019|archive-date=1 July 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190701190856/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/aug/23/catleford-leeds-challenge-cup-final-match-report|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="BBC23Aug14">{{cite web |last1=Scott |first1=Ged |title=Challenge Cup final: Castleford Tigers 10-23 Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28884420 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=23 August 2014 |archive-date=9 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190709095830/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28884420 |url-status=live }}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
[[File:Marc Sneyd Hull FC.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Sneyd in action for Hull at [[Anfield]] in 2019]]
A transfer request in June 2014 was turned down by Salford,<ref name="SS18Jun14">{{cite web |title=Super League: Salford Red Devils reject Marc Sneyd's transfer request |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9353785/super-league-salford-red-devils-reject-marc-sneyds-transfer-request |website=Sky Sports |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=18 June 2014 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707144126/https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9353785/super-league-salford-red-devils-reject-marc-sneyds-transfer-request |url-status=live }}</ref> but soon after, Sneyd signed a three-year contract with [[Hull F.C.]]<ref name="BBC1Jul14">{{cite web |title=Hull FC to sign Leon Pryce & Marc Sneyd |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28107452 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=1 July 2014 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707135957/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/28107452 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="ITV1Jul14">{{cite web |title=Pryce and Sneyd to join Hull FC |url=https://www.itv.com/news/calendar/update/2014-07-01/pryce-and-sneyd-to-join-hull-fc/ |website=ITV |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=1 July 2014 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707135253/https://www.itv.com/news/calendar/update/2014-07-01/pryce-and-sneyd-to-join-hull-fc/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Hull coach [[Lee Radford]] said he "...has a superb left-foot kicking game and a fantastic touch on the ball, which we think will give him the opportunity to blossom into a really good young British half-back."<ref name="BBC1Jul14"/>

In 2016, Sneyd kicked 130 goals and became the league's top goal-kicker.  He won the [[Challenge Cup|2016 Challenge Cup]] with Hull against the [[Warrington Wolves]], kicking two goals in the narrow 12-10 win at [[Wembley Stadium]]; Sneyd was made man-of-the-match and won the prestigious [[Lance Todd Trophy]].<ref name="BBC27Aug16">{{cite web |last1=Scott |first1=Ged |title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves By Ged Scott BBC Sport at Wembley |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=27 August 2016 |archive-date=8 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190708061817/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019|archive-date=28 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191228203836/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|url-status=live}}</ref>

In February 2017, he extended his contract with Hull until 2019.<ref name="SS21Feb17">{{cite web |last1=O'Callaghan |first1=Rory |title=Marc Sneyd signs two-year contract extension with Hull FC |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/10775784/marc-sneyd-signs-two-year-contract-extension-with-hull-fc |website=Sky Sports |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=21 February 2017 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707221211/https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/10775784/marc-sneyd-signs-two-year-contract-extension-with-hull-fc |url-status=live }}</ref> He won the [[2017 Challenge Cup]] with Hull in an 18-14 victory over the record holders [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019|archive-date=6 November 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191106125620/https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|url-status=live}}</ref> and once again he was named the [[Lance Todd Trophy]] winner becoming only the second player to win the award two years in a row.<ref name="SS26Aug17">{{cite web |title=Marc Sneyd delighted to prove doubters wrong |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/11008019/marc-sneyd-delighted-to-prove-doubters-wrong |website=Sky Sports |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=26 August 2017 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707142621/https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/11008019/marc-sneyd-delighted-to-prove-doubters-wrong |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="Gua26Aug17">{{cite web |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Hull beat Wigan to retain Challenge Cup thanks to Marc Sneyd masterclass |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/aug/26/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final-match-report |website=The Guardian |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=26 August 2017 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707133146/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/aug/26/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final-match-report |url-status=live }}</ref> 

In February 2019, Sneyd scored the first ever [[golden point]] in [[Super League]] with a drop goal in a 23–22 win against [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 22-23 Hull FC |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47294114 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=21 March 2024 |date=24 February 2019}}</ref> In April 2019, he signed a new three-year contract until the end of the 2022 season.<ref name="HL16Apr19">{{cite web |last1=Jackson |first1=William |title=Marc Sneyd delighted to have future resolved as he signs long-term Hull FC deal |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/marc-sneyd-delighted-future-resolved-2766196 |website=Hull Live |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=16 April 2019 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707125738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/marc-sneyd-delighted-future-resolved-2766196 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref name="YP16Apr19">{{cite web |last1=Smith |first1=Peter |title=Hull FC delight as Marc Sneyd sticks around until 2022 |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc/hull-fc-delight-as-marc-sneyd-sticks-around-until-2022-1-9716066 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=7 July 2019 |language=en |date=16 April 2019 |archive-date=7 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190707133625/https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc/hull-fc-delight-as-marc-sneyd-sticks-around-until-2022-1-9716066 |url-status=live }}</ref>

In April 2021, Sneyd overtook [[Michael Dobson (rugby league)|Michael Dobson]] in 9th position for the most conversions and penalty goals in [[Super League]] history.

=== Return to Salford===
On 5 November 2021, Sneyd signed a three-year deal to join [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] beginning in the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12460670/super-league-paul-rowley-named-new-salford-red-devils-head-coach-marc-sneyd-returns-from-hull-fc/|title=Paul Rowley named new Salford Red Devils head coach; Marc Sneyd returns from Hull FC|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
In round 3 of the 2022 Super League season, Sneyd was sent to the sin bin in his return game against [[Hull F.C.]] which ended in a 48-16 loss at the [[MKM Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/connor-stars-as-impressive-hull-fc-thrash-red-devils-3587853?amp/|title=Hull FC 48 Salford Red Devils 16: Inspired Jake Connor stars to ruin Marc Sneyd's return to MKM Stadium|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 26 of the 2022 Super League season, Sneyd scored two tries and kicked nine goals in Salford's 50-10 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-10-50-salford-red-devils-highlights-player-ratings-and-talking-points-58230/|title=Castleford Tigers 10-50 Salford Red Devils: Highlights, player ratings and talking points|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>
In round 5 of the 2023 Super League season, Sneyd kicked the winning drop goal in Salford's golden point extra-time victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64953515|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils 14-13 Wakefield Trinity after golden-point extra time|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In the 2023 Super League season, Sneyd played 19 games for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk}}</ref>

===Warrington===
On 13 March 2025, Sneyd joined [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on an immediate eighteen-month deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://warringtonwolves.com/news/2025/march/Marc-Sneyd-joins-the-Wire/ | title=Marc Sneyd Joins the Wire|website=Warrington Wolves | date=13 March 2025 | access-date=13 March 2025}}</ref> He played in the [[2025 Challenge Cup final]] against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and was awarded the Lance Todd Trophy despite being on the losing side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cz9ylzwdxywo |title=Wire were 'better side' in cup final loss - Burgess |work=BBC Sport |date=7 June 2025 |access-date=7 June 2025}}</ref>

==International career==
In October 2013, he was named in the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="BBC3Oct13">{{cite web |title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: Ireland name 24-man squad |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24388679 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=7 July 2019 |date=3 October 2013 |archive-date=9 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190709095834/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24388679 |url-status=live }}</ref> However, despite being eligible through lineage, he was ruled ineligible after failing to complete the paperwork in time to register for the tournament.<ref name="World Cup: James Mendeika will deputise at full-back for Ireland against England">{{cite web|title=World Cup: James Mendeika will deputise at full-back for Ireland against England|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15323/9000990/world-cup-james-mendeika-will-deputise-at-full-back-for-ireland-against-england|website=Sky Sports|access-date=9 April 2021|date=1 November 2013|archive-date=26 September 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140926073724/http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15323/9000990/world-cup-james-mendeika-will-deputise-at-full-back-for-ireland-against-england|url-status=live}}</ref>

Sneyd made his [[England national rugby league team|England]] debut in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 Rugby League World Cup]] match against [[France national rugby league team|France]] in [[Toughsheet Community Stadium|Bolton]] on 22 October 2022, kicking seven goals in England's 42-18 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63360222|title=Rugby League World Cup: England 42-18 France - Shaun Wane's side close to knockouts|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In the third group stage match, Sneyd scored a try and kicked 13 goals in England's 94-4 victory over [[Greece national rugby league team|Greece]] in [[Brammall Lane|Sheffield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63439484|title=Rugby League World Cup: England crush Greece as Dom Young scores four tries|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
